---
title: "ASE Definition Comparison: Site-Level Analysis"

format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: left
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show code"
    theme: cosmo
    embed-resources: true
    fig-width: 9
    fig-height: 6
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
  echo: true
---

# Overview

This document implements the pre-specified analysis for the ASE Definition Comparison study. Sites should run this document from top to bottom without modification. All outputs are saved to the directory specified by `output_directory` in `clif_config.json` for return to the coordinating center.

**Study Question:** Does hospital-level lactate ordering intensity explain the gap between core and extended CDC Adult Sepsis Event (ASE) rates?

**Design:**

- **Exposure:** Lactate ordering intensity (orders per 1,000 patient-days), hospital-year level
- **Outcome:** Two separate binary logistic models on one-row-per-hospitalization data:
  - **Model A:** `sepsis_core` (1) vs `no_sepsis` (0)
  - **Model B:** `sepsis_extended` (1) vs `no_sepsis` (0)
- **Primary estimand:** Contrast between `lactate_vol_scaled` coefficient in Model B minus Model A — does the gap between definitions widen at higher-testing hospitals?
- **Contrast SE:** Conservative approximation `sqrt(Var_B + Var_A)` — assumes independence between models; slightly wider than exact SE since positive cross-model covariance is not subtracted
**Required data file:** `analysis_dataset.parquet` in the `phi_directory` specified in `clif_config.json`


---

# Section 0 — Setup

```{r setup}
library(tidyverse)
library(lme4)       # glmer() — binary logistic with RE (multi-hospital path)
library(sandwich)   # vcovCL() — clustered SEs (single-hospital path)
library(lmtest)     # coeftest()
library(jsonlite)
library(arrow)
library(here)
library(broom)
library(broom.mixed)  # tidy() for glmerMod objects (glmer path)
library(scales)
library(knitr)
library(patchwork)  # combine panels for fig_framing_comparison

set.seed(20240101)

# ── Site configuration (from clif_config.json) ────────────────────────────────
config     <- fromJSON(here("clif_config.json"))
SITE_ID    <- config$site_name
DATA_PATH  <- here(config$phi_directory, "analysis_dataset.parquet")
OUTPUT_DIR <- here(config$output_directory)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Create output directories
dir.create(file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "figures"),     showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
dir.create(file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "tables"),      showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
dir.create(file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "diagnostics"), showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
dir.create(file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "models"),      showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# ── Helper functions ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Fit a single binary logistic model (glmer or glm) with consistent error/warning handling. Returns NULL on error.
# warn_var:   name of variable in parent frame to store convergence warning
fit_binary <- function(formula, data, model_path, warn_var = "fit_warning") {
  assign(warn_var, NULL, envir = parent.frame())

  if (model_path == "glmer") {
    withCallingHandlers(
      tryCatch(
        glmer(formula, data = data, family = binomial,
              control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",
                                     optCtrl   = list(maxfun = 2e5))),
        error = function(e) { warning(e$message); NULL }
      ),
      warning = function(w) {
        assign(warn_var, w$message, envir = parent.frame(3))
        message(warn_var, " WARNING: ", w$message)
        invokeRestart("muffleWarning")
      }
    )
  } else {
    tryCatch(
      glm(formula, data = data, family = binomial),
      error = function(e) { warning(e$message); NULL }
    )
  }
}

# Extract vcov from a fitted model.
# glmer: model-based vcov from GLMM likelihood (fixed effects only).
# glm:   cluster-robust HC0 vcov via sandwich::vcovCL on hospitalization_id.
#        Subsets cluster vector to rows actually used in model fit (NA-safe).
get_model_vcov <- function(model, model_path, data) {
  if (model_path == "glmer") {
    as.matrix(vcov(model))
  } else {
    used_rows <- as.integer(rownames(model.frame(model)))
    sandwich::vcovCL(model,
                     cluster = data$hospitalization_id[used_rows],
                     type    = "HC0")
  }
}

# Tidy a fitted glmer or glm model into a clean data frame.
# Returns NULL if model is NULL.
#   SEs are model-based from the GLMM likelihood; vcov_mat is still returned by get_model_vcov() and used downstream in compute_contrast().

tidy_binary <- function(model, vcov_mat) {
  if (is.null(model)) return(NULL)

  if (inherits(model, "glmerMod")) {
    broom.mixed::tidy(model, effects = "fixed") %>%
      select(term, estimate, std.error, statistic, p.value) %>%
      mutate(conf.low  = estimate - 1.96 * std.error,
             conf.high = estimate + 1.96 * std.error)
  } else {
    broom::tidy(lmtest::coeftest(model, vcov = vcov_mat)) %>%
      mutate(conf.low  = estimate - 1.96 * std.error,
             conf.high = estimate + 1.96 * std.error)
  }
}

# Compute primary contrast from two tidy coefficient tables.
# contrast = lactate_vol_scaled coef(model_b) - coef(model_a)
compute_contrast <- function(coef_a, coef_b, vcov_a, vcov_b,
                             lactate_term = "lactate_vol_scaled") {
  if (is.null(coef_a) || is.null(coef_b)) return(NULL)

  est_a <- coef_a %>% filter(term == lactate_term) %>% pull(estimate)
  est_b <- coef_b %>% filter(term == lactate_term) %>% pull(estimate)

  if (length(est_a) == 0 || length(est_b) == 0) {
    warning("compute_contrast: '", lactate_term, "' not found in one or both models.")
    return(NULL)
  }

  contrast_est <- est_b - est_a

  # SE from vcov diagonal — conservative (ignores cross-model covariance)
  var_a <- tryCatch(vcov_a[lactate_term, lactate_term],
                    error = function(e) NA_real_)
  var_b <- tryCatch(vcov_b[lactate_term, lactate_term],
                    error = function(e) NA_real_)
  contrast_se <- sqrt(var_a + var_b)

  tibble(
    contrast_estimate    = contrast_est,
    contrast_se          = contrast_se,
    contrast_ci_low      = contrast_est - 1.96 * contrast_se,
    contrast_ci_high     = contrast_est + 1.96 * contrast_se,
    contrast_z           = contrast_est / contrast_se,
    contrast_p           = 2 * pnorm(abs(contrast_est / contrast_se),
                                     lower.tail = FALSE),
    se_approximation     = "conservative — cross-model covariance not subtracted",
    lactate_coef_model_a = est_a,
    lactate_coef_model_b = est_b
  )
}

# Add standard metadata columns to a tidy coefficient table
add_metadata <- function(coef_df, model_version, site_id, n_hospitals,
                         n_hospitalizations, lactate_center, lactate_scale,
                         year_center, centering_method, zero_var_covariates,
                         warn_a = NULL, warn_b = NULL) {
  coef_df %>%
    mutate(
      site_id               = site_id,
      model_version         = model_version,
      n_hospitals           = n_hospitals,
      n_hospitalizations    = n_hospitalizations,
      lactate_mean          = lactate_center,
      lactate_sd            = lactate_scale,
      year_center           = year_center,
      centering_method      = centering_method,
      dropped_covariates    = paste(zero_var_covariates, collapse = ", "),
      convergence_warn_a    = !is.null(warn_a),
      convergence_warn_b    = !is.null(warn_b)
    )
}

# Helper: write CSV only if object is non-null and non-empty.
# Emits a loud warning when skipped rather than failing silently — a missing
# export file should always be traceable to a printed reason, not discovered
# later during a pre-distribution review (see SA2b, internal run 2026).
write_if <- function(x, path) {
  if (!is.null(x) && is.data.frame(x) && nrow(x) > 0) {
    write_csv(x, path)
  } else {
    warning("write_if: skipped writing ", basename(path),
            " — object is NULL, not a data frame, or zero-row.")
  }
}

# Extract between-hospital random-intercept variance from a glmer fit.
# Returns a one-row tibble with RE sigma^2 and sigma (SD).
# glm path (single-hospital): returns a stub row with NAs and re_available = FALSE.
# Used for two-level heterogeneity reporting at the coordinating center:
#   - Within-site between-hospital variance (this export, multi-hospital sites)
#   - Between-site variance (from meta-analytic pooling of contrast estimates)
extract_re_variance <- function(model, model_label, model_path, site_id) {
  if (model_path == "glmer" && !is.null(model)) {
    vc      <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(model))
    re_row  <- vc[vc$grp == "hospital_id_deident", ]
    tibble(
      site_id      = site_id,
      model_label  = model_label,
      re_sigma2    = if (nrow(re_row) > 0) re_row$vcov[1]  else NA_real_,
      re_sigma     = if (nrow(re_row) > 0) re_row$sdcor[1] else NA_real_,
      re_available = nrow(re_row) > 0
    )
  } else {
    tibble(
      site_id      = site_id,
      model_label  = model_label,
      re_sigma2    = NA_real_,
      re_sigma     = NA_real_,
      re_available = FALSE
    )
  }
}

# Load data
df_raw <- read_parquet(DATA_PATH)

cat("Data loaded:", nrow(df_raw), "rows,", ncol(df_raw), "columns\n")
cat("Unique hospitalizations:", n_distinct(df_raw$hospitalization_id), "\n")
cat("Unique hospitals:", n_distinct(df_raw$hospital_id), "\n")
```

---

# Section 1 — Data Quality Checks

```{r data-quality}
# 1.1 Overall structure
cat("Rows:", nrow(df_raw), "\n")
cat("Columns:", ncol(df_raw), "\n")
cat("Unique hospitalizations:", n_distinct(df_raw$hospitalization_id), "\n")
cat("Unique hospitals:", n_distinct(df_raw$hospital_id), "\n")

# 1.2 Missing data
missing_summary <- df_raw %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(is.na(.)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "n_missing") %>%
  mutate(pct_missing = round(n_missing / nrow(df_raw) * 100, 1)) %>%
  filter(n_missing > 0)

cat("\nMissing data summary (variables with any missing):\n")
print(missing_summary)

baseline_completeness <- df_raw %>%
  summarise(
    n                     = n(),
    age_miss              = sum(is.na(age)),
    sex_miss              = sum(is.na(sex)),
    cci_miss              = sum(is.na(cci)),
    sofa_miss             = sum(is.na(worst_sofa_admission_to_24h)),
    hosp_type_miss        = sum(is.na(hospital_type)),
    lactate_vol_miss      = sum(is.na(lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days)),
    year_miss             = sum(is.na(year)),
    ASE_miss              = sum(is.na(ASE)),
    defined_w_lactic_miss = sum(is.na(defined_w_lactic)),
    race_eth_miss         = sum(is.na(race_category) |
                                    tolower(race_category) %in%
                                      c("unknown", "not reported", "nan") |
                                    is.na(ethnicity_category) |
                                    tolower(ethnicity_category) %in%
                                      c("unknown", "not reported", "other", "nan")),
    lactate_window_miss   = sum(is.na(lactate_ordered_in_window)),
    los_miss              = sum(is.na(hospital_los_days)),
    in_hosp_death_miss    = sum(is.na(in_hospital_death))
  )

kable(baseline_completeness, caption = "Baseline completeness — key analysis variables")
# Export deferred to Section 6.2 (SITE_ID-prefixed write) — do not write here,
# this was previously duplicating the file under an un-prefixed name.

# 1.3 Structure
cat("\nData structure:\n")
str(df_raw)

# 1.4 Two-row structure check (expected pre-pivot)
row_counts <- df_raw %>%
  count(hospitalization_id) %>%
  count(n, name = "n_encounters")

cat("\nRows per hospitalization_id in raw data (expect all = 2):\n")
kable(row_counts, caption = "Expected: all hospitalizations should have exactly 2 rows")
```

---

# Section 2 — Derived Variables & Wide Format Pivot

```{r analysis-parameters}
# Analysis parameters — modify only with PI approval and SAP amendment ─────────
MIN_HOSP_YEAR_N <- 100  # Minimum hospitalizations per hospital-year for inclusion.
                        # Hospital-years below this threshold are excluded before
                        # all downstream analyses. Pre-specified in SAP §2.3.
cat(paste("Minimum hospital-year N threshold:", MIN_HOSP_YEAR_N, "\n"))
```

```{r derived-variables}
# 2.1 Pivot to wide — one row per hospitalization ──────────────────────────────
# patient_group:
#   sepsis_core         ASE=1 core AND ASE=1 extended (logically required)
#   sepsis_extended     ASE=0 core, ASE=1 extended — captured only by lactate criterion
#   no_sepsis           ASE=0 both definitions
#   check_data_quality  ASE=1 core, ASE=0 extended — logically impossible; flagged then dropped

df_wide <- df_raw %>%
  select(hospitalization_id, hospital_id, defined_w_lactic, ASE,
         lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days, worst_sofa_admission_to_24h,
         age, sex, cci, race_category, ethnicity_category, race_ethnicity,
         hospital_type, year, hospitalizations_from_ed_per_year,
         any_lactate_ordered_whole_hospitalization, lactate_ordered_in_window,
         hospital_los_days, had_icu, icu_los_days, in_hospital_death) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    id_cols      = c(hospitalization_id, hospital_id,
                     lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days,
                     worst_sofa_admission_to_24h, age, sex, cci,
                     race_category, ethnicity_category, race_ethnicity,
                     hospital_type, year, hospitalizations_from_ed_per_year,
                     any_lactate_ordered_whole_hospitalization,
                     lactate_ordered_in_window, hospital_los_days,
                     had_icu, icu_los_days, in_hospital_death),
    names_from   = defined_w_lactic,
    values_from  = ASE,
    names_prefix = "ASE_def_"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    patient_group = case_when(
      ASE_def_0 == 1 & ASE_def_1 == 1 ~ "sepsis_core",
      ASE_def_0 == 0 & ASE_def_1 == 1 ~ "sepsis_extended",
      ASE_def_0 == 0 & ASE_def_1 == 0 ~ "no_sepsis",
      ASE_def_0 == 1 & ASE_def_1 == 0 ~ "check_data_quality",
      TRUE                             ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

cat("Wide format: one row per hospitalization\n")
cat("Total hospitalizations:", nrow(df_wide), "\n")

# 2.2 QC table — flag check_data_quality before exclusion ──────────────────────
n_check_dq <- sum(df_wide$patient_group == "check_data_quality", na.rm = TRUE)
cat(paste("\ncheck_data_quality encounters:", n_check_dq, "\n"))

qc_primary <- df_wide %>%
  mutate(patient_group = factor(patient_group,
                                levels = c("no_sepsis", "sepsis_core",
                                           "sepsis_extended", "check_data_quality"))) %>%
  count(patient_group, .drop = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(
    framing               = "primary_mutually_exclusive",
    pct                   = round(n / sum(n) * 100, 2),
    included_in_analysis  = patient_group != "check_data_quality"
  )

qc_alt <- df_wide %>%
  mutate(patient_group_alt = case_when(
           patient_group == "check_data_quality" ~ "check_data_quality",
           patient_group == "no_sepsis"          ~ "no_sepsis",
           TRUE                                  ~ "ase_extended_any"
         ),
         patient_group_alt = factor(patient_group_alt,
                                    levels = c("no_sepsis", "ase_extended_any",
                                               "check_data_quality"))) %>%
  count(patient_group_alt, .drop = FALSE) %>%
  rename(patient_group = patient_group_alt) %>%
  mutate(
    framing               = "alt_extended_def1",
    pct                   = round(n / sum(n) * 100, 2),
    included_in_analysis  = patient_group != "check_data_quality"
  )

qc_table <- bind_rows(qc_primary, qc_alt) %>%
  select(framing, patient_group, n, pct, included_in_analysis)

kable(qc_table, caption = "Patient group distribution including QC flag (pre-exclusion), primary + alt framing")
write_csv(qc_table,
          file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "diagnostics",
               paste0(SITE_ID, "_02_patient_group_qc.csv")))

if (n_check_dq > 0)
  warning(n_check_dq, " check_data_quality encounters excluded. ",
          "Review data pipeline if unexpectedly large.")

# 2.3 Exclude check_data_quality and set factor levels ─────────────────────────
df <- df_wide %>%
  filter(patient_group != "check_data_quality") %>%
  mutate(
    patient_group  = factor(patient_group,
                            levels = c("no_sepsis", "sepsis_core", "sepsis_extended")),
    # Alt framing grouping, matching Section 4b's alternative Model B definition
    # (ASE_def_1==1 vs no_sepsis): collapses sepsis_core + sepsis_extended into
    # one "meets any extended-definition criterion" group. 
    
    patient_group_alt = factor(
      ifelse(patient_group == "no_sepsis", "no_sepsis", "ase_extended_any"),
      levels = c("no_sepsis", "ase_extended_any")
    ),
    hospital_type  = factor(hospital_type, levels = c("community", "academic")),
    sex            = factor(sex,           levels = c("Male", "Female")),
    year_f         = factor(year),
  
    
    race_eth_model = case_when(
      is.na(ethnicity_category) |
        tolower(ethnicity_category) %in% c("unknown", "not reported",
                                            "other", "nan")           ~ NA_character_,
      is.na(race_category) |
        tolower(race_category) %in% c("unknown", "not reported",
                                       "nan")                          ~ NA_character_,
      tolower(ethnicity_category) == "hispanic"                        ~ "Hispanic",
      tolower(ethnicity_category) %in% c("non-hispanic",
                                          "not hispanic") &
        tolower(race_category) == "white"                              ~ "NH_White",
      tolower(ethnicity_category) %in% c("non-hispanic",
                                          "not hispanic") &
        tolower(race_category) %in% c("black or african american",
                                       "black", "african american",
                                       "black or african-american")    ~ "NH_Black",
      tolower(ethnicity_category) %in% c("non-hispanic",
                                          "not hispanic") &
        tolower(race_category) == "asian"                              ~ NA_character_,
      tolower(ethnicity_category) %in% c("non-hispanic",
                                          "not hispanic")              ~ NA_character_,
      TRUE                                                             ~ NA_character_
    ),
    race_eth_model = factor(race_eth_model,
                            levels = c("NH_White", "NH_Black", "Hispanic"))
  )

cat(paste("Analytic dataset after exclusions:", nrow(df), "hospitalizations\n"))

# race_eth_model distribution check
cat("\nrace_eth_model distribution (derived from race_category + ethnicity_category):\n")
df %>%
  count(race_eth_model, .drop = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(pct = round(n / sum(n) * 100, 1)) %>%
  print(n = 10)

# 2.4 Minimum hospital-year volume threshold ────────────────────────────────────

hosp_year_n <- df %>%
  count(hospital_id, year, name = "n_hosp_year")

excluded_hosp_years <- hosp_year_n %>%
  filter(n_hosp_year < MIN_HOSP_YEAR_N)

if (nrow(excluded_hosp_years) > 0) {
  cat(paste0("\nHospital-year threshold exclusions (N < ",
             MIN_HOSP_YEAR_N, "):\n"))
  cat(paste0("  Excluded: ", nrow(excluded_hosp_years),
             " hospital-year(s), ",
             sum(excluded_hosp_years$n_hosp_year), " hospitalizations\n"))
  cat(paste0("  Retained: ",
             nrow(hosp_year_n) - nrow(excluded_hosp_years),
             " of ", nrow(hosp_year_n), " hospital-years\n"))
  print(excluded_hosp_years)
} else {
  cat(paste0("\nAll hospital-years meet minimum threshold (N >= ",
             MIN_HOSP_YEAR_N, "). No exclusions.\n"))
}

df <- df %>%
  left_join(hosp_year_n, by = c("hospital_id", "year")) %>%
  filter(n_hosp_year >= MIN_HOSP_YEAR_N) %>%
  select(-n_hosp_year)

cat(paste("Analytic dataset after hospital-year threshold:",
          nrow(df), "hospitalizations\n"))

# 2.5 ASE flag consistency cross-check ─────────────────────────────────────────
# Expected patterns:
#   sepsis_extended + window_lactate=0 → IMPOSSIBLE: classified as lactate-only
#     ASE but no lactate drawn in window. Flag as data quality concern.
#   sepsis_extended + window_lactate=1 → Expected ~100% of sepsis_extended.
#   sepsis_core    + window_lactate=0 → Fine: qualified via core criteria; no
#     window lactate drawn or ordered outside window.
#   sepsis_core    + window_lactate=1 → Fine: core criteria met; lactate also
#     drawn in window (overlap patients — both pathways active).
#   no_sepsis      + window_lactate=0 → Structural non-observability: lactate
#     criterion could never have been triggered regardless of result.
#   no_sepsis      + window_lactate=1 → True negative opportunity: lactate
#     drawn in window but result did not meet criterion (or was not elevated).
```

```{r ase-flag-crosscheck}
# Cross-tab: patient_group × lactate_ordered_in_window
flag_crosscheck <- df %>%
  count(patient_group, lactate_ordered_in_window) %>%
  group_by(patient_group) %>%
  mutate(
    pct    = round(n / sum(n) * 100, 1),
    cell   = paste0(n, " (", pct, "%)")
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(patient_group, lactate_ordered_in_window, cell) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from   = lactate_ordered_in_window,
    values_from  = cell,
    names_prefix = "window_lactate_"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    total = df %>%
      count(patient_group) %>%
      pull(n)
  )

kable(flag_crosscheck,
      caption = paste0(
        "ASE group × lactate_ordered_in_window cross-check. ",
        "sepsis_extended patients in window_lactate_0 column indicate a ",
        "data quality concern — lactate-only ASE without window lactate draw."
      ))

# Flag: sepsis_extended patients with no window lactate
n_extended_no_window <- df %>%
  filter(patient_group == "sepsis_extended",
         lactate_ordered_in_window == 0) %>%
  nrow()

if (n_extended_no_window > 0) {
  warning(
    n_extended_no_window,
    " sepsis_extended patients have lactate_ordered_in_window == 0. ",
    "These patients are classified as lactate-only ASE but have no lactate ",
    "draw recorded in the ±2 day ASE window. Review upstream phenotyping — ",
    "possible window definition mismatch between compute_ase() and ",
    "lactate_ordered_in_window derivation."
  )

  
  extended_no_window_diag <- df %>%
    filter(patient_group == "sepsis_extended",
           lactate_ordered_in_window == 0) %>%
    mutate(
      likely_cause = if_else(
        any_lactate_ordered_whole_hospitalization == 1,
        "window_boundary_mismatch_lactate_drawn_elsewhere_in_hosp",
        "no_lactate_order_in_hospitalization_at_all_check_compute_ase"
      )
    ) %>%
    select(hospitalization_id, hospital_id, year,
           any_lactate_ordered_whole_hospitalization,
           lactate_ordered_in_window, likely_cause)

  cat("\nBreakdown of sepsis_extended/no-window-lactate cases by likely cause:\n")
  print(table(extended_no_window_diag$likely_cause))

} else {
  cat("ASE flag check PASSED: all sepsis_extended patients have window lactate draw.\n")
}

# Mechanism stratification summary — no_sepsis subgroups
mech_summary <- df %>%
  filter(patient_group == "no_sepsis") %>%
  count(lactate_ordered_in_window) %>%
  mutate(
    subgroup = case_when(
      lactate_ordered_in_window == 0 ~ "Structural non-observability (lactate not drawn in window)",
      lactate_ordered_in_window == 1 ~ "True negative opportunity (lactate drawn, criterion not met)"
    ),
    pct = round(n / sum(n) * 100, 1)
  ) %>%
  select(subgroup, n, pct)

cat("\nno_sepsis mechanism breakdown:\n")
kable(mech_summary,
      caption = paste0(
        "no_sepsis patients stratified by window lactate observability. ",
        "Structural non-observability = lactate criterion could never have ",
        "been triggered. True negative = lactate drawn but result did not ",
        "meet criterion."
      ))

# Overlap diagnostic: sepsis_core patients with window lactate (both pathways)
n_core_with_window_lactate <- df %>%
  filter(patient_group == "sepsis_core",
         lactate_ordered_in_window == 1) %>%
  nrow()

n_core_total <- sum(df$patient_group == "sepsis_core")

cat(paste0(
  "\nsepsis_core overlap check: ",
  n_core_with_window_lactate, " of ", n_core_total,
  " (", round(n_core_with_window_lactate / n_core_total * 100, 1), "%)",
  " core patients also had lactate drawn in window",
  " — these are the Section 4c overlap group candidates.\n"
))

# 2.6 Collinearity diagnostic: lactate volume vs year ──────────────────────────
# Computed at hospital-year level (one observation per hospital-year).
# Patient-level computation inflates N artificially — every patient in a
# hospital-year shares the same lactate_order value, so the effective N
# is the number of distinct hospital-year combinations, not hospitalizations.
cor_lactate_year_data <- df %>%
  distinct(hospital_id, year, lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days) %>%
  filter(!is.na(lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days))

cor_lactate_year <- cor(cor_lactate_year_data$lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days,
                        cor_lactate_year_data$year)

cat(paste("\nPearson r (lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days ~ year, hospital-year level):",
          round(cor_lactate_year, 3), "\n"))
cat(paste("N hospital-year obs used for r:", nrow(cor_lactate_year_data), "\n"))
if (abs(cor_lactate_year) > 0.7)
  warning("r > 0.7 — strong collinearity. Review SA3 (centered lactate) carefully.")

# 2.7 Hospital count and modeling path ─────────────────────────────────────────
n_hospitals      <- n_distinct(df$hospital_id)
re_structure     <- ifelse(n_hospitals >= 2, "glmer_re",            "glm_clustered_se")
model_path       <- ifelse(n_hospitals >= 2, "glmer",               "glm")
interaction_type <- ifelse(n_hospitals >= 2, "between_hospital",    "within_hospital_temporal")

cat(paste("Number of hospitals:", n_hospitals, "\n"))
cat(paste("Modeling path:", re_structure, "\n"))
cat(paste("Interaction type:", interaction_type, "\n"))

# 2.8 Within-year centering for SA3 ───────────────────────────────────────────
if (n_hospitals >= 2) {
  hospital_year_stats <- df %>%
    distinct(hospital_id, year, lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days) %>%
    group_by(year) %>%
    mutate(
      lactate_volume_year_mean = mean(lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days, na.rm = TRUE),
      lactate_volume_centered  = lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days - lactate_volume_year_mean
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
  centering_method <- "within_year"
} else {
  hospital_year_stats <- df %>%
    distinct(hospital_id, year, lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days) %>%
    mutate(
      lactate_volume_year_mean = mean(lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days, na.rm = TRUE),
      lactate_volume_centered  = lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days - lactate_volume_year_mean
    )
  centering_method <- "grand_mean"
  cat("Single-hospital site: grand-mean centering used for SA3.\n")
}

df <- df %>%
  left_join(
    hospital_year_stats %>%
      select(hospital_id, year, lactate_volume_centered, lactate_volume_year_mean),
    by = c("hospital_id", "year")
  )

n_missing_centered <- sum(is.na(df$lactate_volume_centered))
if (n_missing_centered > 0)
  warning(n_missing_centered, " rows missing lactate_volume_centered after join. ",
          "SA3 will run on reduced sample.")

cat(paste("Centering method:", centering_method, "\n"))

# 2.9 Hospital de-identification ───────────────────────────────────────────────
hosp_crosswalk <- df %>%
  distinct(hospital_id) %>%
  arrange(hospital_id) %>%
  mutate(hospital_id_deident = row_number())

cat("\nHospital de-identification crosswalk (local reference — not exported):\n")
print(hosp_crosswalk)
cat("DO NOT share hospital identity mapping with the coordinating center.\n")

df <- df %>% left_join(hosp_crosswalk, by = "hospital_id")
cat(paste("De-identified IDs assigned:", n_distinct(df$hospital_id_deident), "hospitals\n"))
```

```{r hosp-year-crosstab}
# 2.10 Patient group composition by hospital-year ───────────────────────────────
crosstab_primary <- df %>%
  count(hospital_id_deident, year, patient_group) %>%
  group_by(hospital_id_deident, year) %>%
  mutate(total_hosp_year = sum(n),
         pct             = round(n / total_hosp_year * 100, 1),
         framing         = "primary_mutually_exclusive") %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  rename(group = patient_group)

crosstab_alt <- df %>%
  count(hospital_id_deident, year, patient_group_alt) %>%
  group_by(hospital_id_deident, year) %>%
  mutate(total_hosp_year = sum(n),
         pct             = round(n / total_hosp_year * 100, 1),
         framing         = "alt_extended_def1") %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  rename(group = patient_group_alt)

hosp_year_group_crosstab <- bind_rows(crosstab_primary, crosstab_alt) %>%
  select(hospital_id_deident, year, framing, group, n, total_hosp_year, pct) %>%
  arrange(hospital_id_deident, year, framing, group)

kable(hosp_year_group_crosstab,
      caption = paste0(
        "Patient group composition by hospital-year, primary + alt framing. ",
        "sepsis_extended proportion > 50% of total ASE-positive patients ",
        "is flagged as anomalous (primary framing only, see flag below)."
      ))

# Flag anomalous hospital-years
n_ase_hosp_year <- df %>%
  filter(patient_group != "no_sepsis") %>%
  count(hospital_id_deident, year, patient_group) %>%
  group_by(hospital_id_deident, year) %>%
  mutate(pct_of_ase = n / sum(n) * 100) %>%
  ungroup()

flagged_hosp_years <- n_ase_hosp_year %>%
  filter(patient_group == "sepsis_extended", pct_of_ase > 50)

if (nrow(flagged_hosp_years) > 0) {
  warning(nrow(flagged_hosp_years),
          " hospital-year(s) have sepsis_extended > 50% of total ASE. ",
          "Review upstream phenotyping for these hospital-years.")
  cat("\nFlagged hospital-years:\n")
  print(flagged_hosp_years)
} else {
  cat("Hospital-year group composition check PASSED: no anomalous sepsis_extended proportions.\n")
}

# Export
write_csv(hosp_year_group_crosstab,
          file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "diagnostics",
               paste0(SITE_ID, "_04_patient_group_hosp_year_crosstab.csv")))
```

---

# Section 3 — Descriptive Statistics

```{r descriptives}
# 3.1 Cohort summary
cohort_summary <- df %>%
  summarise(
    n_hospitalizations  = n(),
    n_hospitals         = n_distinct(hospital_id_deident),
    n_years             = n_distinct(year),
    year_range          = paste(min(year), max(year), sep = "-"),
    median_age          = median(age,  na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_female          = round(mean(sex == "Female", na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 1),
    median_cci          = median(cci,  na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_academic        = round(mean(hospital_type == "academic", na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 1),
    n_sepsis_core       = sum(patient_group == "sepsis_core"),
    n_sepsis_extended   = sum(patient_group == "sepsis_extended"),
    n_no_sepsis         = sum(patient_group == "no_sepsis"),
    pct_sepsis_core     = round(mean(patient_group == "sepsis_core")     * 100, 1),
    pct_sepsis_extended = round(mean(patient_group == "sepsis_extended") * 100, 1)
  )

kable(cohort_summary, caption = "Overall cohort summary")

# 3.2 Patient group distribution — long format, primary + alt framing
group_summary_primary <- df %>%
  count(patient_group) %>%
  mutate(pct = round(n / sum(n) * 100, 1),
         framing = "primary_mutually_exclusive") %>%
  rename(group = patient_group)

group_summary_alt <- df %>%
  count(patient_group_alt) %>%
  mutate(pct = round(n / sum(n) * 100, 1),
         framing = "alt_extended_def1") %>%
  rename(group = patient_group_alt)

group_summary <- bind_rows(group_summary_primary, group_summary_alt) %>%
  select(framing, group, n, pct)

kable(group_summary, caption = "Patient group distribution (analytic dataset), primary + alt framing")

# 3.3 ASE rate by year
ase_rate_by_year <- df %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(
    n                   = n(),
    n_sepsis_core       = sum(patient_group == "sepsis_core"),
    n_sepsis_extended   = sum(patient_group == "sepsis_extended"),
    pct_sepsis_core     = round(mean(patient_group == "sepsis_core")     * 100, 1),
    pct_sepsis_extended = round(mean(patient_group == "sepsis_extended") * 100, 1),
    gap_pct_points      = pct_sepsis_extended - pct_sepsis_core,
    .groups             = "drop"
  )

kable(ase_rate_by_year, caption = "ASE rate by year and definition")

# 3.4 Hospital-year lactate volume summary (year-level)
hosp_year_summary <- df %>%
  distinct(hospital_id_deident, year, lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days) %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(
    n_hospitals      = n(),
    mean_lactate_vol = round(mean(lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    sd_lactate_vol   = round(sd(lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days,   na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    min_lactate_vol  = round(min(lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days,  na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    max_lactate_vol  = round(max(lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days,  na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    .groups          = "drop"
  )

kable(hosp_year_summary, caption = "Hospital-year lactate volume summary")

# 3.5 Clinical profile by patient group (sepsis cases only)
# Shared summary builder so primary (sepsis_core, sepsis_extended) and alt
# (ase_extended_any, the combined group) framings compute identical metrics
# from one definition instead of two copies of this block drifting apart.
clinical_profile_summary <- function(data, group_var) {
  data %>%
    group_by({{ group_var }}) %>%
    summarise(
      n                    = n(),
      median_age           = median(age, na.rm = TRUE),
      pct_female           = round(mean(sex == "Female", na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 1),
      median_cci           = median(cci, na.rm = TRUE),
      median_sofa_24h      = median(worst_sofa_admission_to_24h, na.rm = TRUE),
      pct_academic         = round(mean(hospital_type == "academic", na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 1),
      # Race/ethnicity — model levels
      pct_nh_white         = round(mean(race_eth_model == "NH_White",  na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 1),
      pct_nh_black         = round(mean(race_eth_model == "NH_Black",  na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 1),
      pct_hispanic         = round(mean(race_eth_model == "Hispanic",  na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 1),
      # Descriptive only — NH_Asian and NH_Other excluded from adjusted models
      # for cross-site consistency; reported here for Table 1
      pct_nh_asian         = round(mean(!is.na(race_category) &
                                          tolower(ethnicity_category) %in%
                                            c("non-hispanic", "not hispanic") &
                                          tolower(race_category) == "asian",
                                        na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 1),
      pct_nh_other         = round(mean(!is.na(race_category) &
                                          tolower(ethnicity_category) %in%
                                            c("non-hispanic", "not hispanic") &
                                          !tolower(race_category) %in%
                                            c("white", "black or african american",
                                              "black", "african american",
                                              "black or african-american", "asian",
                                              "unknown", "not reported", "nan"),
                                        na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 1),
      pct_race_eth_missing = round(mean(is.na(race_eth_model)) * 100, 1),
      median_hospital_los  = median(hospital_los_days, na.rm = TRUE),
      pct_had_icu          = round(mean(had_icu == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 1),
      median_icu_los       = median(icu_los_days[had_icu == 1], na.rm = TRUE),
      pct_in_hosp_death    = round(mean(in_hospital_death == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 1),
      .groups              = "drop"
    ) %>%
    rename(group = {{ group_var }}) %>%
    pivot_longer(-group, names_to = "metric", values_to = "value")
}

group_clinical_primary <- df %>%
  filter(patient_group %in% c("sepsis_core", "sepsis_extended")) %>%
  clinical_profile_summary(patient_group) %>%
  mutate(framing = "primary_mutually_exclusive")

# Alt framing: the single combined "meets any extended-definition criterion"
# group, for comparison against the primary core/extended split
group_clinical_alt <- df %>%
  filter(patient_group_alt == "ase_extended_any") %>%
  clinical_profile_summary(patient_group_alt) %>%
  mutate(framing = "alt_extended_def1")

group_clinical <- bind_rows(group_clinical_primary, group_clinical_alt) %>%
  select(framing, group, metric, value)

kable(group_clinical, caption = "Clinical profile by patient group (sepsis cases only), primary + alt framing, long format")

# 3.6 Hospital-year lactate detail (one row per hospital per year)
hosp_year_detail <- df %>%
  distinct(hospital_id_deident, year, lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days) %>%
  arrange(hospital_id_deident, year) %>%
  rename(lactate_vol = lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days)

kable(hosp_year_detail, caption = "Hospital-year lactate volume detail (de-identified)")

# 3.7 Hospital-year ASE rates (one row per hospital per year)
# Long format: primary framing carries both patient groups plus the core/extended
# applies with only one non-reference group, so that column is NA for alt rows).
hosp_year_ase_primary <- df %>%
  group_by(hospital_id_deident, year) %>%
  summarise(
    n_hospitalizations = n(),
    n_group             = sum(patient_group == "sepsis_extended"),
    n_group_core        = sum(patient_group == "sepsis_core"),
    pct_group           = round(mean(patient_group == "sepsis_extended") * 100, 1),
    pct_group_core      = round(mean(patient_group == "sepsis_core")     * 100, 1),
    gap_pct_points       = pct_group - pct_group_core,
    .groups              = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(framing = "primary_mutually_exclusive", group = "sepsis_extended")

hosp_year_ase_alt <- df %>%
  group_by(hospital_id_deident, year) %>%
  summarise(
    n_hospitalizations = n(),
    n_group             = sum(patient_group_alt == "ase_extended_any"),
    pct_group           = round(mean(patient_group_alt == "ase_extended_any") * 100, 1),
    .groups              = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(framing = "alt_extended_def1", group = "ase_extended_any",
         n_group_core = NA_integer_, pct_group_core = NA_real_,
         gap_pct_points = NA_real_)

hosp_year_ase <- bind_rows(hosp_year_ase_primary, hosp_year_ase_alt) %>%
  select(hospital_id_deident, year, framing, group, n_hospitalizations,
         n_group, pct_group, n_group_core, pct_group_core, gap_pct_points) %>%
  arrange(hospital_id_deident, year, framing)

kable(hosp_year_ase, caption = "Hospital-year ASE rates, primary + alt framing (de-identified)")
```

```{r fig-lactate-trend, fig.height=5.5}
# 3.8 Figure: Lactate ordering intensity over time
hosp_year_plot_data <- df %>%
  distinct(hospital_id_deident, year, lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days)

site_mean_by_year <- hosp_year_plot_data %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(site_mean = mean(lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop")

ase_rate_plot <- df %>%
  group_by(hospital_id_deident, year) %>%
  summarise(
    rate_core     = mean(patient_group == "sepsis_core",     na.rm = TRUE),
    rate_extended = mean(patient_group == "sepsis_extended", na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

lactate_range <- range(hosp_year_plot_data$lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days, na.rm = TRUE)
scale_factor  <- diff(lactate_range)
scale_offset  <- lactate_range[1]

fig_lactate_trend <- ggplot() +
  geom_line(data  = hosp_year_plot_data,
            aes(x = year, y = lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days,
                group = hospital_id_deident),
            color = "steelblue", alpha = 0.45, linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_point(data = hosp_year_plot_data,
             aes(x = year, y = lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days,
                 group = hospital_id_deident),
             color = "steelblue", alpha = 0.45, size = 1.2) +
  geom_line(data  = site_mean_by_year,
            aes(x = year, y = site_mean),
            color = "steelblue", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(data = site_mean_by_year,
             aes(x = year, y = site_mean),
             color = "steelblue", size = 2.2) +
  geom_line(data  = ase_rate_plot,
            aes(x = year, y = rate_core * scale_factor + scale_offset,
                group = hospital_id_deident),
            color = "firebrick", alpha = 0.45, linewidth = 0.6, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_line(data  = ase_rate_plot,
            aes(x = year, y = rate_extended * scale_factor + scale_offset,
                group = hospital_id_deident),
            color = "darkorange", alpha = 0.45, linewidth = 0.6, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_y_continuous(
    name     = "Lactate Orders per 1,000 Patient-Days",
    sec.axis = sec_axis(transform = ~ (. - scale_offset) / scale_factor,
                        name      = "ASE Rate (proportion)")
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks()) +
  labs(title    = paste0("Lactate Ordering Intensity and ASE Rate Over Time (", SITE_ID, ")"),
       subtitle = "Blue = lactate volume (solid = site mean); Red dashed = core ASE rate; Orange dashed = extended ASE rate",
       x = "Year") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold"),
        panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
        axis.title.y.left  = element_text(color = "steelblue"),
        axis.text.y.left   = element_text(color = "steelblue"),
        axis.title.y.right = element_text(color = "firebrick"),
        axis.text.y.right  = element_text(color = "firebrick"))

ggsave(file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "figures", paste0(SITE_ID, "_fig1_lactate_ase_trend.png")),
       plot = fig_lactate_trend, width = 9, height = 5.5, dpi = 300)
fig_lactate_trend
```

```{r fig-ase-gap}
# 3.9 Figure: ASE rate gap over time
ase_gap_by_hosp_year <- df %>%
  group_by(hospital_id_deident, year) %>%
  summarise(
    pct_core     = mean(patient_group == "sepsis_core",     na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    pct_extended = mean(patient_group == "sepsis_extended", na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    gap          = pct_extended - pct_core,
    .groups      = "drop"
  )

fig_ase_gap <- ggplot() +
  { if (n_hospitals > 1)
    geom_line(data  = ase_gap_by_hosp_year,
              aes(x = year, y = gap, group = hospital_id_deident),
              color = "grey65", alpha = 0.5, linewidth = 0.6)
  } +
  geom_line(data  = ase_rate_by_year,
            aes(x = year, y = gap_pct_points),
            color = "darkorchid", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(data = ase_rate_by_year,
             aes(x = year, y = gap_pct_points),
             color = "darkorchid", size = 2.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = scales::pretty_breaks()) +
  labs(title    = paste0("Extended vs Core ASE Rate Gap Over Time (", SITE_ID, ")"),
       subtitle = "Gap = extended minus core ASE rate (pp)\nGrey = individual hospitals; Purple = site mean",
       x = "Year", y = "ASE Rate Gap (percentage points)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"), panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

ggsave(file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "figures", paste0(SITE_ID, "_fig2_ase_rate_gap.png")),
       plot = fig_ase_gap, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)
fig_ase_gap
```

```{r fig-group-dist}
# 3.10 Figure: Patient group distribution

fig_group_dist <- group_summary %>%
  filter(framing == "primary_mutually_exclusive") %>%
  mutate(patient_group = factor(group,
                                levels = c("no_sepsis", "sepsis_core", "sepsis_extended"),
                                labels = c("No Sepsis", "Core Sepsis", "Extended Only"))) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = patient_group, y = pct, fill = patient_group)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(pct, "%\n(n=", scales::comma(n), ")")),
            vjust = -0.4, size = 3.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("No Sepsis"     = "grey75",
                               "Core Sepsis"   = "firebrick",
                               "Extended Only" = "darkorange")) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.12))) +
  labs(title    = paste0("Patient Group Distribution (", SITE_ID, ")"),
       subtitle = paste0("N = ", scales::comma(nrow(df)), " unique encounters"),
       x = NULL, y = "Percentage of Encounters (%)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold"),
        panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.major.x = element_blank())

ggsave(file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "figures", paste0(SITE_ID, "_fig3_patient_group_distribution.png")),
       plot = fig_group_dist, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)
fig_group_dist
```

```{r fig-clinical-profile}
# 3.11 Figure: Clinical profile comparison
# group_clinical is long format (framing x group x metric x value) as of the
# alt-framing update — this figure compares the two primary-framing groups
# (sepsis_core vs sepsis_extended); the alt framing's single combined group
# has nothing to contrast against here.
group_clinical_primary_wide <- group_clinical %>%
  filter(framing == "primary_mutually_exclusive",
         metric %in% c("median_age", "pct_female", "median_cci", "median_sofa_24h")) %>%
  pivot_wider(id_cols = group, names_from = metric, values_from = value)

if (nrow(group_clinical_primary_wide) == 2) {
  fig_clinical_profile <- group_clinical_primary_wide %>%
    mutate(patient_group = factor(group,
                                  levels = c("sepsis_core", "sepsis_extended"),
                                  labels = c("Core Sepsis", "Extended Only"))) %>%
    select(patient_group, median_age, pct_female, median_cci, median_sofa_24h) %>%
    pivot_longer(-patient_group, names_to = "variable", values_to = "value") %>%
    mutate(variable = recode(variable,
                             "median_age"     = "Median Age (years)",
                             "pct_female"     = "Female (%)",
                             "median_cci"     = "Median CCI",
                             "median_sofa_24h" = "Median SOFA (0-24h)")) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = value, y = variable, color = patient_group, shape = patient_group)) +
    geom_point(size = 4) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Core Sepsis"   = "firebrick",
                                  "Extended Only" = "darkorange")) +
    labs(title    = paste0("Clinical Profile: Core vs Extended-Only Sepsis (", SITE_ID, ")"),
         subtitle = "Median values; Extended-only = meets ASE criteria via lactate criterion only",
         x = "Value", y = NULL, color = NULL, shape = NULL) +
    facet_wrap(~ variable, scales = "free_x", ncol = 2) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
    theme(plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold"),
          panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
          legend.position  = "bottom",
          strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold"))

  ggsave(file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "figures", paste0(SITE_ID, "_fig4_clinical_profile.png")),
         plot = fig_clinical_profile, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
  fig_clinical_profile
} else {
  cat("NOTE: Clinical profile figure skipped — one or both sepsis groups absent.\n")
  fig_clinical_profile <- NULL
}
```

---

# Section 4 — Primary Analysis

Two separate binary logistic models. Both fit on the wide-format dataset (one row per hospitalization). `sepsis_extended` cases excluded from Model A; `sepsis_core` cases excluded from Model B.

- **Multi-hospital:** `glmer` with `(1 | hospital_id_deident)`, model-based SEs
- **Single-hospital:** `glm` with sandwich cluster-robust SEs on `hospitalization_id`
- **Primary contrast:** `lactate_vol_scaled` coefficient, Model B minus Model A

```{r primary-analysis}
# 4.1 Prepare model dataset
year_center <- round(mean(df$year, na.rm = TRUE), 2)

df_model <- df %>%
  mutate(
    lactate_vol_scaled = scale(lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days)[, 1],
    year_centered      = year - year_center
  )

lactate_center <- round(attr(scale(df$lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days),
                              "scaled:center"), 2)
lactate_scale  <- round(attr(scale(df$lactate_order_per_1000_patient_days),
                              "scaled:scale"),  2)
cat(paste("Lactate scaling: mean =", lactate_center, "| SD =", lactate_scale, "\n"))
cat(paste("Year centering offset:", year_center, "\n"))

# 4.2 Zero-variance covariate check
zero_var_covariates <- c("worst_sofa_admission_to_24h", "age", "sex", "cci",
                         "race_eth_model", "hospital_type", "year_centered",
                         "hospitalizations_from_ed_per_year") %>%
  purrr::keep(~ {
    col <- df_model[[.x]]
    if (is.factor(col)) nlevels(droplevels(col)) < 2
    else length(unique(col[!is.na(col)])) < 2
  })

if (length(zero_var_covariates) > 0) {
  warning("Zero-variance covariates dropped: ",
          paste(zero_var_covariates, collapse = ", "))
} else {
  cat("No zero-variance covariates detected.\n")
}

covariate_terms <- setdiff(
  c("worst_sofa_admission_to_24h", "age", "sex", "cci",
    "race_eth_model", "hospital_type", "year_centered",
    "hospitalizations_from_ed_per_year"),
  zero_var_covariates
)

# 4.3 Build formulas
# Model A: sepsis_core vs no_sepsis (excludes sepsis_extended)
# Model B: sepsis_extended vs no_sepsis (excludes sepsis_core)
# RE term added only for glmer path
re_term        <- if (model_path == "glmer") "(1 | hospital_id_deident)" else NULL
fixed_rhs      <- c("lactate_vol_scaled", covariate_terms, re_term)
formula_model_a <- reformulate(fixed_rhs, response = "outcome_a")
formula_model_b <- reformulate(fixed_rhs, response = "outcome_b")

cat(paste("Model formula RHS:", deparse(reformulate(fixed_rhs)), "\n"))
cat(paste("Modeling path:", re_structure, "\n"))

# 4.4 Prepare binary outcome datasets
df_model_a <- df_model %>%
  filter(patient_group %in% c("no_sepsis", "sepsis_core")) %>%
  mutate(outcome_a = as.integer(patient_group == "sepsis_core"))

df_model_b <- df_model %>%
  filter(patient_group %in% c("no_sepsis", "sepsis_extended")) %>%
  mutate(outcome_b = as.integer(patient_group == "sepsis_extended"))

cat(paste("Model A sample (core vs no_sepsis): n =",
          nrow(df_model_a), "hospitalizations\n"))
cat(paste("Model B sample (extended vs no_sepsis): n =",
          nrow(df_model_b), "hospitalizations\n"))

# 4.5 Fit models
warn_primary_a <- NULL
warn_primary_b <- NULL

model_a <- fit_binary(formula_model_a, df_model_a, model_path,
                      warn_var = "warn_primary_a")
model_b <- fit_binary(formula_model_b, df_model_b, model_path,
                      warn_var = "warn_primary_b")

cat(paste("Model A fit:", ifelse(is.null(model_a), "FAILED", "OK"), "\n"))
cat(paste("Model B fit:", ifelse(is.null(model_b), "FAILED", "OK"), "\n"))

# 4.6 Extract coefficients
vcov_a <- if (!is.null(model_a)) get_model_vcov(model_a, model_path, df_model_a) else NULL
vcov_b <- if (!is.null(model_b)) get_model_vcov(model_b, model_path, df_model_b) else NULL

coef_a_raw <- tidy_binary(model_a, vcov_a)
coef_b_raw <- tidy_binary(model_b, vcov_b)

metadata_args <- list(
  site_id            = SITE_ID,
  n_hospitals        = n_hospitals,
  n_hospitalizations = nrow(df_model),
  lactate_center     = lactate_center,
  lactate_scale      = lactate_scale,
  year_center        = year_center,
  centering_method   = centering_method,
  zero_var_covariates = zero_var_covariates,
  warn_a             = warn_primary_a,
  warn_b             = warn_primary_b
)

coef_a <- if (!is.null(coef_a_raw))
  do.call(add_metadata, c(list(coef_df = coef_a_raw,
                                model_version = paste0(re_structure, "_model_a_core_vs_nosepsis")),
                           metadata_args))

coef_b <- if (!is.null(coef_b_raw))
  do.call(add_metadata, c(list(coef_df = coef_b_raw,
                                model_version = paste0(re_structure, "_model_b_extended_vs_nosepsis")),
                           metadata_args))

# Combine for export
coef_primary <- bind_rows(
  if (!is.null(coef_a)) mutate(coef_a, model_label = "model_a_core_vs_nosepsis"),
  if (!is.null(coef_b)) mutate(coef_b, model_label = "model_b_extended_vs_nosepsis")
)

if (nrow(coef_primary) > 0) {
  cat("\nPrimary model — lactate_vol_scaled coefficients:\n")
  kable(coef_primary %>%
          filter(term == "lactate_vol_scaled") %>%
          select(model_label, estimate, std.error, statistic, p.value,
                 conf.low, conf.high),
        digits = 4,
        caption = "Primary models — lactate_vol_scaled by model (log-OR scale)")
}

# 4.7 Primary contrast
contrast_primary <- compute_contrast(coef_a_raw, coef_b_raw, vcov_a, vcov_b)

if (!is.null(contrast_primary)) {
  contrast_primary <- contrast_primary %>%
    mutate(
      site_id            = SITE_ID,
      model_version      = re_structure,
      n_hospitals        = n_hospitals,
      n_hospitalizations = nrow(df_model),
      blup_available     = (model_path == "glmer"),
      convergence_warn_a = !is.null(warn_primary_a),
      convergence_warn_b = !is.null(warn_primary_b)
    )
  cat("\nPrimary contrast (Model B minus Model A lactate coefficient):\n")
  kable(contrast_primary, digits = 4,
        caption = "Primary estimand — contrast on lactate_vol_scaled")
} else {
  warning("Primary contrast could not be computed — one or both models failed.")
}
```

```{r hospital-level-export}
# 4.8 Hospital-level BLUP export
# Multi-hospital: BLUPs from glmer ranef() for both models.
# Single-hospital: one-row stub, blup_available = FALSE.
# All IDs de-identified. No hospital metadata exported.

if (model_path == "glmer" &&
    !is.null(model_a) && !is.null(model_b) &&
    !is.null(contrast_primary)) {

  blup_df <- tryCatch({
    blup_a <- ranef(model_a)$hospital_id_deident %>%
      rownames_to_column("hospital_id_deident") %>%
      rename(blup_a = `(Intercept)`) %>%
      mutate(hospital_id_deident = as.integer(hospital_id_deident))

    blup_b <- ranef(model_b)$hospital_id_deident %>%
      rownames_to_column("hospital_id_deident") %>%
      rename(blup_b = `(Intercept)`) %>%
      mutate(hospital_id_deident = as.integer(hospital_id_deident))

    hosp_n <- df_model %>%
      group_by(hospital_id_deident) %>%
      summarise(n_hospitalizations_hospital = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
      mutate(hospital_id_deident = as.integer(hospital_id_deident))

    blup_a %>%
      left_join(blup_b, by = "hospital_id_deident") %>%
      left_join(hosp_n, by = "hospital_id_deident") %>%
      mutate(
        fixef_contrast         = contrast_primary$contrast_estimate,
        # Hospital-specific contrast = fixef contrast + blup_b - blup_a
        hosp_contrast_estimate = fixef_contrast + blup_b - blup_a,
        blup_available         = TRUE,
        blup_se_note           = "approximate — posterior variance not extracted; use for forest plot only",
        site_id                = SITE_ID,
        model_version          = re_structure
      )
  },
  error = function(e) {
    warning("BLUP extraction failed: ", e$message)
    NULL
  })

} else if (model_path == "glm" && !is.null(contrast_primary)) {

  blup_df <- tibble(
    hospital_id_deident         = 1L,
    blup_a                      = NA_real_,
    blup_b                      = NA_real_,
    fixef_contrast              = contrast_primary$contrast_estimate,
    hosp_contrast_estimate      = contrast_primary$contrast_estimate,
    n_hospitalizations_hospital = nrow(df_model),
    blup_available              = FALSE,
    blup_se_note                = "single-hospital — no shrinkage; use fixef_contrast for forest plot",
    site_id                     = SITE_ID,
    model_version               = re_structure
  )

} else {
  blup_df <- NULL
  warning("Hospital-level export could not be generated.")
}

if (!is.null(blup_df)) {
  cat("\nHospital-level export:\n")
  kable(blup_df, digits = 4,
        caption = "Hospital-level estimates (de-identified)")
}

# 4.9 RE variance export (primary models) ──────────────────────────────────────
# Between-hospital random intercept variance from glmer (multi-hospital path).
# Single-hospital path returns NA stubs so all sites return this file uniformly.
# Coordinating center combines within-site RE variance (this file) with
# between-site variance from meta-analytic pooling for two-level heterogeneity.
re_var_primary <- bind_rows(
  extract_re_variance(model_a, "model_a_core_vs_nosepsis",     model_path, SITE_ID),
  extract_re_variance(model_b, "model_b_extended_vs_nosepsis", model_path, SITE_ID)
)

cat("\nBetween-hospital RE variance — primary models:\n")
print(re_var_primary)
```

---

# Section 4b — Alternative Contrast: Non-Mutually-Exclusive Extended Framing

```{r alt-framing-check}
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# This section fits an alternative Model B to produce a directionality check
# on the primary contrast estimand.
#
# PRIMARY framing (Section 4):
#   Model A: sepsis_core      vs no_sepsis  (positive group = ASE_def_0==1)
#   Model B: sepsis_extended  vs no_sepsis  (positive group = lactate-only marginal,
#                                            ASE_def_0==0 AND ASE_def_1==1)
#   Contrast = Beta_B - Beta_A
#   Answers: does lactate ordering intensity predict reclassification into
#            the marginal lactate-only group?
#
# ALTERNATIVE framing (this section):
#   Model A: identical to primary — no refitting needed; reuses model_a / vcov_a
#   Model B: ASE_def_1==1 vs no_sepsis (positive group = sepsis_core + sepsis_extended,
#            i.e., everyone meeting the 6-criterion extended definition)
#   Contrast = Beta_B_alt - Beta_A
#   Answers: does lactate ordering intensity widen the overall gap between the
#            extended and core definitions? (policy-level question)
#
# Comparing the two contrasts reveals whether the direction is stable across
# how the extended group is bounded. If both are the same sign, the directional
# finding is not sensitive to the mutual-exclusivity assumption. If they diverge,
# the marginal lactate-only cases are driving a different signal than the
# combined extended population.
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

cat("Fitting alternative Model B: ASE_def_1==1 (sepsis_core + sepsis_extended) vs no_sepsis\n")
cat("Model A reused from Section 4 without refitting.\n\n")

# Full analytic cohort; outcome = 1 for anyone meeting the 6-criterion definition
df_model_b_alt <- df_model %>%
  mutate(outcome_b_alt = as.integer(patient_group != "no_sepsis"))

cat(paste("Alternative Model B: n =", nrow(df_model_b_alt), "hospitalizations\n"))
cat(paste("  Positive (sepsis_core + sepsis_extended): n =",
          sum(df_model_b_alt$outcome_b_alt), "\n"))
cat(paste("  Negative (no_sepsis): n =",
          sum(df_model_b_alt$outcome_b_alt == 0), "\n"))

formula_model_b_alt <- reformulate(fixed_rhs, response = "outcome_b_alt")

warn_alt_b  <- NULL
model_b_alt <- fit_binary(formula_model_b_alt, df_model_b_alt, model_path,
                           warn_var = "warn_alt_b")

cat(paste("Alternative Model B fit:", ifelse(is.null(model_b_alt), "FAILED", "OK"), "\n"))

vcov_b_alt     <- if (!is.null(model_b_alt))
  get_model_vcov(model_b_alt, model_path, df_model_b_alt) else NULL
coef_b_alt_raw <- tidy_binary(model_b_alt, vcov_b_alt)

coef_b_alt <- if (!is.null(coef_b_alt_raw))
  add_metadata(
    coef_df             = coef_b_alt_raw,
    model_version       = paste0(re_structure, "_alt_model_b_extended_def1_vs_nosepsis"),
    site_id             = SITE_ID,
    n_hospitals         = n_hospitals,
    n_hospitalizations  = nrow(df_model_b_alt),
    lactate_center      = lactate_center,
    lactate_scale       = lactate_scale,
    year_center         = year_center,
    centering_method    = centering_method,
    zero_var_covariates = zero_var_covariates,
    warn_a              = warn_primary_a,
    warn_b              = warn_alt_b
  )

# Fold alt-framing coefs into the primary coefs export (one file per analysis —
# primary and alt framing are both "the primary analysis", just different
# Model B definitions sharing the same Model A) instead of a separate file.
if (!is.null(coef_b_alt))
  coef_primary <- bind_rows(coef_primary, mutate(coef_b_alt, model_label = "model_b_alt"))

# Alternative contrast — reuses vcov_a from Section 4 (no new variance needed for Model A)
contrast_alt <- compute_contrast(coef_a_raw, coef_b_alt_raw, vcov_a, vcov_b_alt)

if (!is.null(contrast_alt)) {
  contrast_alt <- contrast_alt %>%
    mutate(
      site_id            = SITE_ID,
      model_version      = paste0(re_structure, "_alt_extended_def1_vs_nosepsis"),
      n_hospitals        = n_hospitals,
      n_hospitalizations = nrow(df_model_b_alt),
      blup_available     = (model_path == "glmer"),
      convergence_warn_a = !is.null(warn_primary_a),
      convergence_warn_b = !is.null(warn_alt_b),
      framing            = "alt_extended_def1"
    )
}

# Append alt-model RE variance to the primary RE variance export (defined in
# Section 4.9, before this Model B_alt fit existed)
re_var_primary <- bind_rows(
  re_var_primary,
  extract_re_variance(model_b_alt, "model_b_alt_extended_def1_vs_nosepsis", model_path, SITE_ID)
)

# Alternative Model B lactate coefficient
if (!is.null(coef_b_alt)) {
  cat("\nAlternative Model B — lactate_vol_scaled coefficient:\n")
  kable(
    coef_b_alt %>%
      filter(term == "lactate_vol_scaled") %>%
      select(term, estimate, std.error, statistic, p.value, conf.low, conf.high),
    digits  = 4,
    caption = "Alternative Model B (ASE_def_1==1 vs no_sepsis) — lactate coefficient (log-OR scale)"
  )
}

# Side-by-side directionality check
if (!is.null(contrast_primary) && !is.null(contrast_alt)) {
  contrast_comparison <- bind_rows(
    contrast_primary %>%
      select(contrast_estimate, contrast_se, contrast_ci_low, contrast_ci_high,
             contrast_p, lactate_coef_model_a, lactate_coef_model_b) %>%
      mutate(framing = "primary: lactate-only (sepsis_extended) vs no_sepsis",
             .before = 1),
    contrast_alt %>%
      select(contrast_estimate, contrast_se, contrast_ci_low, contrast_ci_high,
             contrast_p, lactate_coef_model_a, lactate_coef_model_b) %>%
      mutate(framing = "alternative: all extended (ASE_def_1==1) vs no_sepsis",
             .before = 1)
  )

  cat("\nPrimary vs alternative contrast — directionality check:\n")
  kable(
    contrast_comparison,
    digits  = 4,
    caption = paste0(
      "Directionality check: consistent sign across both framings indicates the ",
      "direction is not driven by how the extended group is bounded. ",
      "Divergence in sign or magnitude indicates the lactate-only marginal cases ",
      "behave differently from the combined extended population with respect to ",
      "lactate ordering intensity."
    )
  )
} else {
  warning("Directionality check skipped — one or both contrasts unavailable.")
}

# Figure: framing comparison — coefficient panel + contrast forest panel ──────
fig_framing_comparison <- NULL

if (!is.null(contrast_primary) && !is.null(contrast_alt) &&
    !is.null(coef_a_raw) && !is.null(coef_b_raw) && !is.null(coef_b_alt_raw)) {

  # Panel A data: Model A and Model B lactate coefficients under each framing.
  # Model A is identical across both framings (no refit) — shown once per
  # framing row for visual symmetry with Model B, which does differ.
  panel_a_data <- bind_rows(
    coef_a_raw %>%
      filter(term == "lactate_vol_scaled") %>%
      mutate(framing = "Primary",     model = "Model A (sepsis_core)"),
    coef_b_raw %>%
      filter(term == "lactate_vol_scaled") %>%
      mutate(framing = "Primary",     model = "Model B (sepsis_extended)"),
    coef_a_raw %>%
      filter(term == "lactate_vol_scaled") %>%
      mutate(framing = "Alternative", model = "Model A (sepsis_core)"),
    coef_b_alt_raw %>%
      filter(term == "lactate_vol_scaled") %>%
      mutate(framing = "Alternative", model = "Model B (ASE_def_1==1)")
  ) %>%
    mutate(
      framing = factor(framing, levels = c("Primary", "Alternative")),
      # Reverse levels so Model A (anchor) appears at top of y-axis
    model   = factor(model, levels = c("Model B (ASE_def_1==1)",
                                          "Model B (sepsis_extended)",
                                          "Model A (sepsis_core)"))
    )

  panel_a <- ggplot(panel_a_data,
                     aes(x = estimate, y = model, color = framing)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
    geom_pointrange(
      aes(xmin = estimate - 1.96 * std.error,
          xmax = estimate + 1.96 * std.error),
      position = position_dodge(width = 0.4), size = 0.6
    ) +
    labs(
      title = "Model-level lactate coefficients by framing",
      x     = "Log-OR (lactate ordering intensity)",
      y     = NULL,
      color = "Framing"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
    theme(legend.position = "top")

  # Panel B: contrast forest — primary vs alternative
  panel_b_data <- contrast_comparison %>%
    mutate(
      framing_short = if_else(str_detect(framing, "^primary"),
                               "Primary\n(lactate-only vs no_sepsis)",
                               "Alternative\n(all extended vs no_sepsis)"),
      framing_short = factor(framing_short,
                              levels = c("Alternative\n(all extended vs no_sepsis)",
                                         "Primary\n(lactate-only vs no_sepsis)"))
    )

  panel_b <- ggplot(panel_b_data,
                     aes(x = contrast_estimate, y = framing_short)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
    geom_pointrange(
      aes(xmin = contrast_ci_low, xmax = contrast_ci_high),
      size = 0.7, color = "black"
    ) +
    labs(
      title = "Cross-model contrast (β_B − β_A)",
      x     = "Contrast estimate, log-OR scale (95% CI)",
      y     = NULL
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

  fig_framing_comparison <- panel_a / panel_b +
    plot_annotation(
      title    = paste0(SITE_ID, ": Primary vs alternative extended-definition framing"),
      subtitle = "Consistent sign across framings = directional stability. Magnitude attenuation in alternative framing reflects sepsis_core dilution."
    )

  ggsave(file.path(OUTPUT_DIR, "figures",
                    paste0(SITE_ID, "_fig5_framing_comparison.png")),
         plot = fig_framing_comparison, width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 300)

  fig_framing_comparison
} else {
  warning("fig_framing_comparison skipped — one or more required model objects unavailable.")
}
```


---

# Section 5 — Sensitivity Analyses

```{r sensitivity-sa1}
# SA1: Restrict to encounters with lactate ordered in the ASE window ────────────
cat("Fitting SA1: restrict to lactate ordered in ASE window (window-specific)\n")

df_sa1_a <- df_model %>%
  filter(lactate_ordered_in_window == 1,
         patient_group %in% c("no_sepsis", "sepsis_core")) %>%
  mutate(outcome_a = as.integer(patient_group == "sepsis_core"))

df_sa1_b <- df_model %>%
  filter(lactate_ordered_in_window == 1,
         patient_group %in% c("no_sepsis", "sepsis_extended")) %>%
  mutate(outcome_b = as.integer(patient_group == "sepsis_extended"))

cat(paste("SA1 Model A: n =", nrow(df_sa1_a),
          "| Model B: n =", nrow(df_sa1_b), "\n"))

warn_sa1_a <- NULL; warn_sa1_b <- NULL
model_sa1_a <- fit_binary(formula_model_a, df_sa1_a, model_path, "warn_sa1_a")
model_sa1_b <- fit_binary(formula_model_b, df_sa1_b, model_path, "warn_sa1_b")

vcov_sa1_a <- if (!is.null(model_sa1_a)) get_model_vcov(model_sa1_a, model_path, df_sa1_a) else NULL
vcov_sa1_b <- if (!is.null(model_sa1_b)) get_model_vcov(model_sa1_b, model_path, df_sa1_b) else NULL

coef_sa1 <- bind_rows(
  if (!is.null(model_sa1_a)) tidy_binary(model_sa1_a, vcov_sa1_a) %>%
    mutate(model_label = "model_a", model_version = "sa1_lactate_ordered_model_a",
           site_id = SITE_ID, n_rows = nrow(df_sa1_a)),
  if (!is.null(model_sa1_b)) tidy_binary(model_sa1_b, vcov_sa1_b) %>%
    mutate(model_label = "model_b", model_version = "sa1_lactate_ordered_model_b",
           site_id = SITE_ID, n_rows = nrow(df_sa1_b))
)

contrast_sa1 <- compute_contrast(
  tidy_binary(model_sa1_a, vcov_sa1_a),
  tidy_binary(model_sa1_b, vcov_sa1_b),
  vcov_sa1_a, vcov_sa1_b
)

if (!is.null(contrast_sa1))
  contrast_sa1 <- contrast_sa1 %>%
    mutate(site_id = SITE_ID, model_version = "sa1_lactate_ordered")

re_var_sa1 <- bind_rows(
  extract_re_variance(model_sa1_a, "model_a_core_vs_nosepsis",     model_path, SITE_ID),
  extract_re_variance(model_sa1_b, "model_b_extended_vs_nosepsis", model_path, SITE_ID)
)

cat("SA1 complete.\n")
if (nrow(coef_sa1) > 0)
  kable(coef_sa1 %>% filter(term == "lactate_vol_scaled") %>%
          select(model_label, estimate, std.error, p.value, conf.low, conf.high),
        digits = 4, caption = "SA1 — lactate_vol_scaled by model")
if (!is.null(contrast_sa1))
  kable(contrast_sa1, digits = 4, caption = "SA1 — primary contrast")

# SA1 alt framing — same lactate-ordered-in-window restriction, full cohort,
# outcome = ASE_def_1==1 vs no_sepsis. Model A reused from SA1 above (no refit).
df_sa1_b_alt <- df_model %>%
  filter(lactate_ordered_in_window == 1) %>%
  mutate(outcome_b_alt = as.integer(patient_group_alt == "ase_extended_any"))

warn_sa1_b_alt  <- NULL
model_sa1_b_alt <- fit_binary(formula_model_b_alt, df_sa1_b_alt, model_path, "warn_sa1_b_alt")
vcov_sa1_b_alt  <- if (!is.null(model_sa1_b_alt))
  get_model_vcov(model_sa1_b_alt, model_path, df_sa1_b_alt) else NULL
coef_sa1_b_alt_raw <- tidy_binary(model_sa1_b_alt, vcov_sa1_b_alt)

if (!is.null(coef_sa1_b_alt_raw))
  coef_sa1 <- bind_rows(
    coef_sa1,
    coef_sa1_b_alt_raw %>%
      mutate(model_label = "model_b_alt", model_version = "sa1_lactate_ordered_model_b_alt",
             site_id = SITE_ID, n_rows = nrow(df_sa1_b_alt))
  )

contrast_sa1_alt <- compute_contrast(
  tidy_binary(model_sa1_a, vcov_sa1_a),
  coef_sa1_b_alt_raw, vcov_sa1_a, vcov_sa1_b_alt
)
if (!is.null(contrast_sa1_alt))
  contrast_sa1_alt <- contrast_sa1_alt %>%
    mutate(site_id = SITE_ID, model_version = "sa1_lactate_ordered_alt",
           n_hospitalizations = nrow(df_sa1_b_alt))

re_var_sa1 <- bind_rows(
  re_var_sa1,
  extract_re_variance(model_sa1_b_alt, "model_b_alt_extended_def1_vs_nosepsis", model_path, SITE_ID)
)

cat("SA1 alt framing complete.\n")
```

```{r sensitivity-sa2a}
# SA2a: Exclude COVID years (2020–2021) ────────────────────────────────────────
cat("Fitting SA2a: exclude COVID years 2020-2021\n")

df_sa2a_a <- df_model %>%
  filter(!year %in% c(2020, 2021),
         patient_group %in% c("no_sepsis", "sepsis_core")) %>%
  mutate(outcome_a = as.integer(patient_group == "sepsis_core"))

df_sa2a_b <- df_model %>%
  filter(!year %in% c(2020, 2021),
         patient_group %in% c("no_sepsis", "sepsis_extended")) %>%
  mutate(outcome_b = as.integer(patient_group == "sepsis_extended"))

if (nrow(df_sa2a_a) == 0) {
  cat("SA2a: No data outside 2020-2021 — skipping.\n")
  coef_sa2a <- NULL; contrast_sa2a <- NULL; re_var_sa2a <- NULL
  contrast_sa2a_alt <- NULL
} else {
  cat(paste("SA2a Model A: n =", nrow(df_sa2a_a),
            "| Model B: n =", nrow(df_sa2a_b), "\n"))

  warn_sa2a_a <- NULL; warn_sa2a_b <- NULL
  model_sa2a_a <- fit_binary(formula_model_a, df_sa2a_a, model_path, "warn_sa2a_a")
  model_sa2a_b <- fit_binary(formula_model_b, df_sa2a_b, model_path, "warn_sa2a_b")

  vcov_sa2a_a <- if (!is.null(model_sa2a_a)) get_model_vcov(model_sa2a_a, model_path, df_sa2a_a) else NULL
  vcov_sa2a_b <- if (!is.null(model_sa2a_b)) get_model_vcov(model_sa2a_b, model_path, df_sa2a_b) else NULL

  coef_sa2a <- bind_rows(
    if (!is.null(model_sa2a_a)) tidy_binary(model_sa2a_a, vcov_sa2a_a) %>%
      mutate(model_label = "model_a", model_version = "sa2a_exclude_covid_model_a",
             site_id = SITE_ID, years_excluded = "2020, 2021", n_rows = nrow(df_sa2a_a)),
    if (!is.null(model_sa2a_b)) tidy_binary(model_sa2a_b, vcov_sa2a_b) %>%
      mutate(model_label = "model_b", model_version = "sa2a_exclude_covid_model_b",
             site_id = SITE_ID, years_excluded = "2020, 2021", n_rows = nrow(df_sa2a_b))
  )

  contrast_sa2a <- compute_contrast(
    tidy_binary(model_sa2a_a, vcov_sa2a_a),
    tidy_binary(model_sa2a_b, vcov_sa2a_b),
    vcov_sa2a_a, vcov_sa2a_b
  )
  if (!is.null(contrast_sa2a))
    contrast_sa2a <- contrast_sa2a %>%
      mutate(site_id = SITE_ID, model_version = "sa2a_exclude_covid")

  re_var_sa2a <- bind_rows(
    extract_re_variance(model_sa2a_a, "model_a_core_vs_nosepsis",     model_path, SITE_ID),
    extract_re_variance(model_sa2a_b, "model_b_extended_vs_nosepsis", model_path, SITE_ID)
  )

  cat("SA2a complete.\n")
  if (nrow(coef_sa2a) > 0)
    kable(coef_sa2a %>% filter(term == "lactate_vol_scaled") %>%
            select(model_label, estimate, std.error, p.value, conf.low, conf.high),
          digits = 4, caption = "SA2a — lactate_vol_scaled by model")
  if (!is.null(contrast_sa2a))
    kable(contrast_sa2a, digits = 4, caption = "SA2a — primary contrast")

  # SA2a alt framing — same COVID-year exclusion, full cohort, outcome = ASE_def_1==1
  # vs no_sepsis. Model A reused from SA2a above (no refit).
  df_sa2a_b_alt <- df_model %>%
    filter(!year %in% c(2020, 2021)) %>%
    mutate(outcome_b_alt = as.integer(patient_group_alt == "ase_extended_any"))

  warn_sa2a_b_alt  <- NULL
  model_sa2a_b_alt <- fit_binary(formula_model_b_alt, df_sa2a_b_alt, model_path, "warn_sa2a_b_alt")
  vcov_sa2a_b_alt  <- if (!is.null(model_sa2a_b_alt))
    get_model_vcov(model_sa2a_b_alt, model_path, df_sa2a_b_alt) else NULL
  coef_sa2a_b_alt_raw <- tidy_binary(model_sa2a_b_alt, vcov_sa2a_b_alt)

  if (!is.null(coef_sa2a_b_alt_raw))
    coef_sa2a <- bind_rows(
      coef_sa2a,
      coef_sa2a_b_alt_raw %>%
        mutate(model_label = "model_b_alt", model_version = "sa2a_exclude_covid_model_b_alt",
               site_id = SITE_ID, years_excluded = "2020, 2021", n_rows = nrow(df_sa2a_b_alt))
    )

  contrast_sa2a_alt <- compute_contrast(
    tidy_binary(model_sa2a_a, vcov_sa2a_a),
    coef_sa2a_b_alt_raw, vcov_sa2a_a, vcov_sa2a_b_alt
  )
  if (!is.null(contrast_sa2a_alt))
    contrast_sa2a_alt <- contrast_sa2a_alt %>%
      mutate(site_id = SITE_ID, model_version = "sa2a_exclude_covid_alt",
             n_hospitalizations = nrow(df_sa2a_b_alt))

  re_var_sa2a <- bind_rows(
    re_var_sa2a,
    extract_re_variance(model_sa2a_b_alt, "model_b_alt_extended_def1_vs_nosepsis", model_path, SITE_ID)
  )

  cat("SA2a alt framing complete.\n")
}
```

```{r sensitivity-sa2b}
# SA2b: Year-by-year stratification (exploratory) ──────────────────────────────
cat("Fitting SA2b: year-by-year stratified models\n")

years_available <- sort(unique(df_model$year))
cat(paste("SA2b years:", paste(years_available, collapse = ", "), "\n"))

sa2b_results <- map(years_available, function(yr) {
  d_a <- df_model %>%
    filter(year == yr, patient_group %in% c("no_sepsis", "sepsis_core")) %>%
    mutate(outcome_a = as.integer(patient_group == "sepsis_core"))
  d_b <- df_model %>%
    filter(year == yr, patient_group %in% c("no_sepsis", "sepsis_extended")) %>%
    mutate(outcome_b = as.integer(patient_group == "sepsis_extended"))
  # Alt framing: full within-year cohort (all three patient_group levels),
  # outcome = ASE_def_1==1 vs no_sepsis
  d_b_alt <- df_model %>%
    filter(year == yr) %>%
    mutate(outcome_b_alt = as.integer(patient_group_alt == "ase_extended_any"))

  cat(paste("SA2b year", yr, ": Model A n =", nrow(d_a),
            "| Model B n =", nrow(d_b), "| Model B_alt n =", nrow(d_b_alt), "\n"))

  # Drop zero-var covariates within year (year_centered always zero within a year)
  yr_zero_var <- c("hospital_type", "sex", "year_centered") %>%
    purrr::keep(~ {
      col <- d_a[[.x]]
      if (is.factor(col)) nlevels(droplevels(col)) < 2
      else length(unique(col[!is.na(col)])) < 2
    })
  yr_rhs <- c("lactate_vol_scaled",
               setdiff(covariate_terms, c(yr_zero_var, "year_centered")),
               if (model_path == "glmer") "(1 | hospital_id_deident)" else NULL)
  yr_formula_a     <- reformulate(yr_rhs, response = "outcome_a")
  yr_formula_b     <- reformulate(yr_rhs, response = "outcome_b")
  yr_formula_b_alt <- reformulate(yr_rhs, response = "outcome_b_alt")

  warn_yr_a <- NULL; warn_yr_b <- NULL; warn_yr_b_alt <- NULL
  fit_a     <- fit_binary(yr_formula_a,     d_a,     model_path, "warn_yr_a")
  fit_b     <- fit_binary(yr_formula_b,     d_b,     model_path, "warn_yr_b")
  fit_b_alt <- fit_binary(yr_formula_b_alt, d_b_alt, model_path, "warn_yr_b_alt")

  if (is.null(fit_a) && is.null(fit_b) && is.null(fit_b_alt))
    warning("SA2b year ", yr, ": all three models (A, B, B_alt) failed to fit — ",
            "no coefs/contrast will be produced for this year. Check n and ",
            "covariate variance within year.")

  # A single failed model is enough to null out its contrast even when the
  # others succeed — flag that case specifically since it wouldn't otherwise
  # be visible until contrast_sa2b/contrast_sa2b_alt turn up empty downstream.
  if (is.null(fit_a) && (!is.null(fit_b) || !is.null(fit_b_alt)))
    warning("SA2b year ", yr, ": Model A failed to fit while Model B/B_alt ",
            "succeeded — primary and/or alt contrast for this year will be NA.")
  if (is.null(fit_b) && !is.null(fit_a))
    warning("SA2b year ", yr, ": Model B (primary framing) failed to fit — ",
            "primary contrast for this year will be NA.")
  if (is.null(fit_b_alt) && !is.null(fit_a))
    warning("SA2b year ", yr, ": Model B_alt (alt framing) failed to fit — ",
```
